extracted_json.title: HW #1, Prob #1 — Applying the SPSP to "Improve the existing cell-phone"

blocks.text: Problem as given : "Improve the existing cell-phone."
Apply the SPSP to this problem
Step 1 : Define the (real) problem (requires reflection, thinking, asking questions)

items.text: SP1 : Establish customer needs that all (generic) cell-phones should satisfy
SP2 : Assess existing cell-phone products to determine how well these customer needs are satisfied
SP3 : Identify (generate) ways (or solutions) to improve existing cell-phones w.r.t. (a) customer needs (from SP1) & assessment (from SP2)

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Applying the SPSP method to define and decompose the vague problem of improving an existing cell-phone.
extracted_json.summary_gist: The page works HW #1, Prob #1, where the given problem ("Improve the existing cell-phone") is identified as vague and open-ended. Step 1 of the SPSP is to define the real problem, which requires reflection and questioning, illustrated with a rough conceptual sketch (Customer Needs → Assess → Improve). The problem is then broken into three sub-problems: SP1 establishes generic customer needs, SP2 assesses how well existing products satisfy them, and SP3 identifies ways to improve cell-phones with respect to SP1's needs and SP2's assessment.
